content:
Key Findings

  • West Lancashire Borough Council spent £332.9M across 43,063 transactions between 2016/17 and 2025/26
  • Total spending increased by £18.1M (62.9%) from 2017 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £29.9M (118.9%) from 2019 to 2020
  • The largest year-over-year decrease was £25.5M (55.4%) from 2021 to 2022
  • The council transacted with 4,614 unique suppliers over the 10-year period

West Lancashire's Spending Trends

West Lancashire Borough Council's spending has fluctuated significantly over the past decade. The total expenditure of £332.9M across 43,063 transactions reveals a complex financial landscape. The most notable increase occurred between 2019 and 2020, where spending surged by £29.9M (118.9%). This sharp rise was followed by a substantial decrease of £25.5M (55.4%) from 2021 to 2022, highlighting the volatility in the council's financial commitments.

The overall increase of £18.1M (62.9%) from 2017 to 2025 suggests a trend of growing expenditure. However, the year-over-year data shows that this growth is not linear. For instance, the council saw a significant drop of £10.8M (30.1%) from 2018 to 2019, followed by another decrease of £8.9M (16.1%) from 2020 to 2021. These fluctuations indicate periods of both increased and decreased financial activity, which may reflect changes in council priorities or external economic conditions.

Transparency and Supplier Diversity

West Lancashire Borough Council's transparency efforts are evident in the publication of 43,063 transactions over a decade. This level of detail allows for a comprehensive analysis of the council's spending patterns. The council transacted with 4,614 unique suppliers, demonstrating a diverse range of vendors. This diversity is crucial for ensuring fair competition and preventing monopolistic practices.

The council's spending data provides valuable insights into its financial operations. For example, the significant increase in spending from 2019 to 2020 could be attributed to various factors, such as increased service demands or infrastructure projects. Conversely, the sharp decrease from 2021 to 2022 might reflect cost-saving measures or a reduction in certain services. Understanding these patterns can help residents and stakeholders better comprehend the council's financial decisions.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to West Lancashire Borough Council about the specific reasons behind the significant spending fluctuations between 2019 and 2022
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to gain insights into the council's financial planning and decision-making processes
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see detailed information about the suppliers and the nature of the transactions
  • Write to your local councillor about the transparency of the council's spending data and request more detailed explanations for the observed trends
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis of West Lancashire Borough Council's financial transactions
This article is based on West Lancashire Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
